name: Comprehensive GST & Vendor Compliance Audit (2026)
description: Standard CA firm audit for Input Tax Credit (ITC) eligibility, Blocked Credits under Sec 17(5), and MSME payment compliance.

checks.focus: Extract the Vendor GSTIN, Purchaser GSTIN, and the Invoice Date.
checks.rule: Both GSTINs must be exactly 15 alphanumeric characters. The invoice date must be clearly stated. Flag if any are missing or invalid.

checks.focus: Extract the exact nature of the goods or services provided in the invoice or contract.
checks.rule: Evaluate if the items fall under Blocked Credits (Section 17(5) of CGST Act). Flag as a severe violation if the goods include: motor vehicles for passenger transport, food and beverages, outdoor catering, beauty treatment, health services, cosmetic surgery, or membership of a club/health/fitness centre.

checks.focus: Extract the Invoice Reference Number (IRN) and look for the presence of a QR code.
checks.rule: For B2B transactions, an IRN (64-character hash) and QR code are mandatory if the vendor's turnover exceeds the mandate. If neither an IRN nor QR code is mentioned on the invoice, flag this as a potential e-invoicing compliance risk.

checks.focus: Extract the payment terms, specifically the number of days within which payment must be made to the vendor.
checks.rule: Under Section 43B(h) of the Income Tax Act (impacting audit), payments to Micro/Small enterprises must be made within 45 days (if a written agreement exists) or 15 days (if no agreement). If the stated payment terms exceed 45 days, flag it as a violation.
